title: Option 1 — webinar lead ingestion upsert payload
description: One payload per registrant, upserted downstream. Upsert-on-key makes the pipeline idempotent (re-running the same export changes nothing). Required vs nullable is driven by the real data: name blank in 31/83, phone blank in 52/83.

dedup_key.description: Person identity = normalized email when an '@' is present; else a per-REGISTRATION surrogate (corporate-domain anchor + stable row hash) for contact-less rows. A bare domain is NEVER the key on its own: two distinct registrants can share it (e.g. vitalant.org) and an upsert on the shared key would destructively merge them, erasing signal. The corporate domain is kept separately in _meta.account_domain as a non-destructive account hint. Always present (every row gets a stable key -> idempotent re-ingest).

object.description: Which Salesforce object(s) this row writes. The pipeline NEVER creates Accounts/Contacts (read-only for matching); those exist only at human conversion.

lead.description: Person record. Null for contact-less rows (bare domain, no person) — those become an Account Task, not a Lead.

company.description: Lead.Company (text). Not an Account object — Account is created at conversion.

phone_e164.description: E.164, written ONLY when trustworthy (quality ok/repaired); null otherwise, so a rep never dials a bad number. The raw value + quality flag are retained in _meta (phone_raw/phone_quality) — nulling the CRM field is not discarding data. Region hint = the ISO country column, never the email TLD.

lifecycle.description: Hint from the file, resolved against Salesforce (SOR wins). Both-blank defaults to Lead.

owner.description: Resolved owner or queue/pod. For net-new, SF round-robin sets the final rep.

confidence_flag.description: Categorical, derived from the match path (not a fabricated numeric score); downgraded by failed email verification.

routing_situation.description: Stamped by the pipeline; a Salesforce flow reads it on assignment to fire the right task.

campaign_member.description: Webinar attendance. Unique on (person + webinar_id) -> idempotent; re-ingest never duplicates.

level.description: keyword = a hit on the brief's content-trap term set (validate AI QMS / Veeva / MasterControl / replace) -> elevated to Priority Tier 1; none = no trap term. On this data: 1 hit in 83 — our measured count on the file, not a figure the brief states — the 'validate AI QMS' evaluation question; 0 competitor/displacement mentions. The matched term(s) are in `keywords`, from which the competitor-vs-evaluation type is read. Topical AI interest without a trap term is NOT escalated.

_meta.description: Operational metadata. Stays in the audit log / output CSV — NOT pushed to Salesforce, except confidence_flag (rep-facing). Provisioning any custom SF field is a documented deploy step.

consent_status.description: Lawful-basis gate (matters: the buyer sells compliance). Source = registration form/CRM, not this export.

account_domain.description: Corporate domain for account-level aggregation (a non-destructive hint). Null for consumer/no-domain rows. NEVER used as a merge key.

shared_domain_collision.description: True when >=2 contact-less rows share this corporate domain — distinct registrants that must not be merged; routed to human review.

company_conflict.description: Detector: on a contact-less corporate-bare row, True = the email domain and Organization name different companies (the rigged file's poison column) -> confidence downgraded to low, human review. False = they corroborate. Null = not applicable (named row, consumer/no domain, or blank/junk Organization). A detector, NOT a resolver: we never pick the 'true' company (the shuffle destroyed it).

org_junk.description: True when a non-blank Organization yields no usable company-name token (e.g. a phone number in the org field, row 81: '8123255522'). The field is then ignored for domain corroboration (company_conflict is null) AND as an enrichment company hint; the flag records the org was junk, not merely absent. Bright line = all-digits (str.isdigit), never a 'real name?' judgment (that is the resolver this build refuses). Audit-only, never a Salesforce field.

phone_quality.description: Phone normalization outcome. ok/repaired = trustworthy E.164 (written to SF); repaired = a deterministic trunk-prefix fix was applied (e.g. AU parenthetical '(02)'). no_country_code/junk/too_short/blank = not written to SF (null), raw kept in phone_raw; an absent dial on an ICP row rides the enrichment loop. The flag lives here in the audit record, NOT as a Salesforce custom field.

phone_raw.description: The original phone string as it arrived (pre-normalization), retained for traceability so nulling the CRM field never destroys source data.
